steps.title: Identify the grammatical structure on each side
steps.content:
Left side: “*Georgics*, an epic poem of Roman origin, dates back to the [date]” — an independent clause.
Right side: “the poem's heroic narrative still captivates modern readers” — an independent clause.

steps.title: Apply the rule for joining independent clauses
steps.content:
The rule: a comma and a coordinating conjunction join two independent clauses.

Choice A applies this rule correctly—the comma precedes “yet,” which signals the contrast between the ancient date and the poem's modern appeal.

The other choices violate this rule by either reversing the comma-conjunction order, omitting the conjunction, or removing all punctuation.

That is the correct answer.
